scenes.name: atlas_puppet
scenes.worldSetup: World::create(); Atlas loaded via DartLoader from dart://sample/sdf/atlas/atlas_v3_no_head.urdf plus a code-built ground skeleton (WeldJoint, 10x10x0.01 box at z=-0.005, Color::Blue(0.2)) and a black 0.25*(1,1,0.5) visual-only box on the Atlas root for easier click-drag. Z-up, purely kinematic: viewer.allowSimulation(false), no gravity/stepping used. setupStartConfiguration(): squat both legs (hpy -45deg, kny +90deg, aky -45deg), arms posed (shx +/-65, ely 90, elx -/+90, wry 65), knee lower limits set to 10deg. setupEndEffectors(): l_hand/r_hand EndEffectors (relative tf (0.0009,0.1254,0.012) rotZ 90deg, mirrored for right) with InteractiveFrame targets lh_target/rh_target, whole-body IK, root-joint gradient weights 0.01, infinite linear/angular bounds; l_foot/r_foot EndEffectors (tf (0.186,0,-0.08)) with lf_target/rf_target, IK hierarchy level 1, linear z bound 1e-8 and angular x/y bounds 1e-8 (feet flat on ground), rectangular SupportGeometry, support active; robot dropped so feet touch ground (dof 5), foot targets snapped to foot transforms. setupWholeBodySolver(): GradientDescentSolver max 10 iterations; RelaxedPosture objective (weights 0.01 with root rot-z/pos-x/pos-y zeroed and rot-x/rot-y/pos-z scaled 0.2; posture box [-0.35,0.35] on root rx/ry, pos-z [0.600,0.885], [-0.1,0.1] on dofs 6-8); BalanceConstraint added as eq-constraint index 1 with FROM_CENTROID + SHIFT_SUPPORT (commented alternates: SHIFT_COM, OPTIMIZE_BALANCE, FROM_EDGE). SupportPolygonVisual attachment at elevation 0.05. Window 1280x960 (scaled by --gui-scale), camera home (5.34,3.00,2.41)->(0,0,1) up (-0.20,-0.08,0.98).
scenes.controllers: TeleoperationWorld : dart::gui::osg::WorldNode. customPreRefresh() (NOT customPreStep — runs per render frame since sim is disabled): if any move flag set, composes a root FreeJoint delta from body-frame forward/left (z-flattened, normalized) and world Z: linearStep 0.01, elevationStep 0.002, rotationalStep 2deg per refresh, then setPositions(FreeJoint::convertToPositions(new_tf)); always calls mAtlas->getIK(true)->solveAndApply(true) each frame; counts consecutive failures and prints "Failing!" once at iter==1000. State: vector<bool> mMoveComponents (Q,W,E,A,S,D,F,Z), mAnyMovement. InputHandler (osgGA::GUIEventHandler) holds rest config, per-EE default bounds/target transforms/active flags, pointers to RelaxedPosture objective and BalanceConstraint (fetched via getIK()->getProblem()->getEqConstraint(1)), mOptimizationKey='r'.
keyBindings: w (keydown/keyup): move robot forward along body x, 0.01 m/frame
s (keydown/keyup): move robot backward
a (keydown/keyup): move robot left along body y
d (keydown/keyup): move robot right
q (keydown/keyup): rotate robot CCW about world Z, 2 deg/frame
e (keydown/keyup): rotate robot CW about world Z
f (keydown/keyup): raise elevation, 0.002 m/frame
z (keydown/keyup): lower elevation, 0.002 m/frame
r (hold): RelaxedPosture.enforceIdealPosture=true + BalanceConstraint OPTIMIZE_BALANCE; on release restore false + FROM_CENTROID
t: reset all DOFs to rest config except root yaw/x/y (skeleton indices 2,3,4 preserved)
p: print every DOF name and position to stdout

1-9: toggle IK target InteractiveFrame for EndEffector #(key-1); 4 exist in order l_hand, r_hand, l_foot, r_foot (keys 1-4); on activate: standard bounds + target snapped to EE + frame added to world; on deactivate: custom default bounds/target restored + frame removed
x: toggle l_foot Support active
c: toggle r_foot Support active
(lowercase only — uppercase variants are NOT handled in this example, unlike hubo_puppet)
tunables: --gui-scale <factor> CLI (GuiScaleOptions; also -h/--help prints usage and exits)
no ImGui panel; runtime-tunable only via keys (support toggles, EE target toggles, posture-optimize hold)

compile-time constants a port may want to surface: linearStep 0.01 / elevationStep 0.002 / rotationalStep 2deg, GradientDescentSolver max iterations 10, RelaxedPosture weights and posture box bounds, BalanceConstraint error/balance method pair (FROM_CENTROID+SHIFT_SUPPORT), support polygon display elevation 0.05
scenes.interactions: viewer.enableDragAndDrop(bodyNode,false,false) on every Atlas BodyNode (Alt+click translate, Ctrl+click rotate, Shift+click move via parent joint — printed instructions); enableDragAndDrop on each EE IK target that is an InteractiveFrame (drag the 3-ring/arrow gizmos to drive whole-body IK); SupportPolygonVisual shows green support polygon, blue/red COM ball, green centroid ball; instructions printed via viewer.getInstructions() plus a custom usage block.
scenes.portRisks: Kinematic-only scene: relies on viewer.allowSimulation(false) and drives everything from customPreRefresh (render-rate, not step-rate) — in a consolidated host with a shared viewer this global flag and per-frame IK must be scoped per scene. InputHandler is a raw osgGA handler added to the Viewer; must be removed on scene switch or its w/a/s/d/q/e/... keys leak into other demos. BalanceConstraint retrieved by hard index getEqConstraint(1) — fragile if solver setup order changes. IK modules/EndEffectors are created on the skeleton and persist with it. SupportPolygonVisual is a viewer attachment needing teardown. Key set overlaps heavily with vehicle and atlas_simbicon demos. Needs dart-gui-osg + dart-utils-urdf (CMake components utils-urdf gui-osg, DART >= 6.17).

scenes.name: atlas_simbicon
scenes.worldSetup: Y-UP world: new World, gravity (0,-9.81,0). Ground from dart://sample/sdf/atlas/ground.urdf via DartLoader; Atlas from dart://sample/sdf/atlas/atlas_v3_no_head.sdf via SdfParser::readSkeleton; atlas->setPosition(0,-pi/2) to stand it up in the Y-up frame. AtlasSimbiconWorldNode : RealTimeWorldNode (real-time stepping). node->showShadow(): default shadow technique cast to osgShadow::ShadowMap with 4096x4096 texture. ImGuiViewer + AtlasSimbiconWidget + AtlasSimbiconEventHandler. Window 1280x960 scaled, viewer->realize() then window renamed "Atlas Simbicon", camera home (5.14,3.28,6.28)*2 -> (1,0,0), up (0,0.1,0). Controller built in node ctor: joint damping 80.0 on every joint dof (except root joint index 0), initial configuration (positions+velocities) snapshot for reset, 4 state machines built: "standing" (1 state, PD hold posture), "walking in place" (4 states), "walking" (4 states), "running" (2 states, 0.15s timer); DEFAULT current machine is mStateMachines[1] = walking in place, begun at t=0.
scenes.controllers: AtlasSimbiconWorldNode::customPreStep(): adds mExternalForce to pelvis via addExtForce, calls Controller::update(), decrements mForceDuration else zeroes force. Controller::update(): only if isAllowingControl() (pelvis world-Y within [-0.70, 0.30] — emergency cutoff), calls current StateMachine::computeControlForce(dt): checks TerminalCondition (TimerCondition elapsed>duration, or BodyContactCondition BodyNode::isColliding — deprecated API), transitions to next state, then State::computeControlForce: Simbicon balance feedback thetad = desired + d*cd + v*cv (sagittal/coronal COM measured in a COM frame built with UnitY ground normal), PD torque tau[i] = -Kp(q-thetad) - Kd*dq with Kp=1e3, Kd=1.0, first 6 root dofs zeroed, then _updateTorqueForStanceLeg (global-frame torso/swing-hip compensation), skeleton->setForces. Gains per machine: cd=0.5, cv=0.2 on hip dofs (l/r_leg_hpx coronal, l/r_leg_hpy sagittal). StateMachine name->label mapping used by the node: switchToNormalStrideWalking->"walking", switchToShortStrideWalking->"running", switchToNoControl->"standing". reset(): zero external force + restore initial Skeleton configuration. Push helpers default force=500 N for 100 frames (+x fwd, -x back, +z left, -z right). Controller also implements harnessPelvis/LeftFoot/RightFoot via WeldJointConstraint and a keyboard(h,j,k,r,n,1-4) method that is NOT wired to any event handler (dead code).
keyBindings: r / R: reset Atlas to initial configuration and clear external force
a / A: push pelvis forward (+x, 500 N for 100 sim frames)
s / S: push pelvis backward (-x, 500 N for 100 frames)
d / D: push pelvis left (+z, 500 N for 100 frames)
f / F: push pelvis right (-z, 500 N for 100 frames)

(unwired Controller::keyboard exists with h: harness pelvis toggle, j: harness left foot, k: harness right foot, r: reset, n: force next state, 1: standing, 2: walking in place, 3: walking, 4: running — never registered, port decision needed)
tunables: ImGui SliderFloat 'Gravity Acc.' 5.0-20.0 (applied as -value * UnitY)
ImGui Play/Pause radio (viewer->simulate)
ImGui Checkbox 'Headlights On/Off'
ImGui Checkbox 'Shadow On/Off' (node showShadow/hideShadow)
ImGui Checkbox 'Depth mode' (CameraMode::DEPTH vs RGBA)
ImGui Button 'Reset Atlas'
ImGui RadioButtons control mode: 0 'No Control' -> standing SM, 1 'Short-Stride Walking' -> running SM, 2 'Normal-Stride Walking' -> walking SM (default selection 2)
--gui-scale CLI
compile-time: push force 500/frames 100, Kp 1e3, Kd 1.0, cd 0.5, cv 0.2, joint damping 80, pelvis height cutoff [-0.70,0.30], per-state desired joint angles
scenes.interactions: No drag-and-drop, no mouse picking. ImGui control panel (window 'Atlas Control', 360x400, menu bar with Exit/About DART, Help section listing the r/a/s/d/f keys). Keyboard pushes via osgGA event handler.
scenes.portRisks: Y-up world: gravity, camera up-vector, gravity slider (-Y), and the Simbicon COM frame (UnitY normal in State::getCOMFrame) all assume Y-up — cannot share a Z-up host grid/camera without care. showShadow() reads mViewer so node must already be attached to a viewer; shadow + headlight + depth-mode state is per-viewer, needs reset on scene switch. Widget label/state-machine mismatch: 'Short-Stride Walking' actually selects the 'running' machine. BodyContactCondition uses deprecated BodyNode::isColliding (DART_SUPPRESS_DEPRECATED) and requires collision results from stepping. Raw owning pointers (StateMachine*/State*/TerminalCondition* with manual delete). Controller keyboard()/harness functionality unwired — parity decision. harnessLeftFoot adds no constraint to the solver (bug: constraint created but never added). viewer->realize() + setWindowName assumes standalone window. Default state machine on load is 'walking in place' although GUI radio shows mode 2 (Normal-Stride) — GUI only syncs after first user change (mControlMode initialized to 2 without invoking the switch).

scenes.name: hubo_puppet
scenes.worldSetup: Z-up kinematic scene (viewer.allowSimulation(false)); new World. DRC-Hubo loaded with DartLoader after loader.addPackageDirectory("drchubo", DART_DATA_PATH "/urdf/drchubo") from DART_DATA_PATH "/urdf/drchubo/drchubo.urdf" (compile-time filesystem path, NOT a dart:// URI); all finger bodies removed (BodyNodes named Body_LF*/Body_RF* removed in a loop). Startup config: LHP/RHP -45deg, LKP/RKP 90deg, LAP/RAP -45deg, LSP/RSP 30deg, LEP/REP -120deg; LSY/LWY/RSY/RWY limits clamped to +/-90deg. setupEndEffectors(): l_hand/r_hand on Body_LWR/Body_RWR (tf z -0.09) with InteractiveFrame targets, whole-body IK, analytical HuboArmIK gradient method (base Body_LSP/Body_RSP, Matt Zucker derivation), POST_ANALYTICAL extra-dof utilization, extra error clamp 0.1, root weights 0.01 (7-dim!), infinite bounds; l_foot/r_foot on Body_LAR/Body_RAR (tf (0.14,0,-0.126) incl ground_dist 0.01) with HuboLegIK (base Body_LHY/Body_RHY), hierarchy level 1, linear z bound 1e-8 offset by ground_offset, angular x/y 1e-8, rectangular support geometry, support ACTIVE; l_peg/r_peg on Body_LWP/Body_RWP (tf z +0.09) with HuboArmIK, single-point support geometry created but NOT active. Robot then dropped to ground (rootJoint_pos_z), foot targets snapped. After EE setup the skeleton is CLONED (cloneSkeleton("hubo_copy")) and the clone is used (tests IK cloning); positions reapplied. Code-built ground box (same as atlas_puppet). setupWholeBodySolver(): RelaxedPosture objective (weights 0.01, root rot-z/x/y zeroed; posture box root rx [-0.35,0.35], ry [-0.35,0.50], pos-z [0.55,0.95]); BalanceConstraint eq-constraint index 1, FROM_CENTROID + SHIFT_SUPPORT; GradientDescentSolver max 5 iterations. SupportPolygonVisual elevation 0.05. Window 1280x960 scaled; camera (5.34,3.00,1.91)->(0,0,0.50) up (-0.20,-0.08,0.98).
scenes.controllers: TeleoperationWorld : WorldNode. customPreRefresh(): same root FreeJoint move logic as atlas_puppet (linearStep 0.01, elevationStep 0.002, rotationalStep 2deg) but with public mAmplifyMovement flag that doubles all three steps while Left-Shift is held; then mHubo->getIK(true)->solveAndApply(true) every frame (no failure counter output). InputHandler mirrors atlas_puppet: stores rest config, per-EE default bounds/targets/active flags for all 6 EEs, RelaxedPosture and BalanceConstraint pointers (getEqConstraint(1)), mOptimizationKey='r'. HuboArmIK/HuboLegIK are custom InverseKinematics::Analytical subclasses (8 closed-form solution branches each, joint-limit checked) defined in this file and registered per-EE via setGradientMethod<T>; they lazily configure() against skeleton geometry (Body_TSY pelvis) and are cloneable.
keyBindings: w / W (keydown/keyup): move forward (0.01 m/frame; 0.02 with Shift held)
s / S (keydown/keyup): move backward
a / A (keydown/keyup): move left
d / D (keydown/keyup): move right
q / Q (keydown/keyup): rotate CCW about Z (2 deg/frame; 4 with Shift)
e / E (keydown/keyup): rotate CW about Z
f / F (keydown/keyup): raise elevation (0.002 m/frame; 0.004 with Shift)
z / Z (keydown/keyup): lower elevation
Left Shift (keydown/keyup): amplify movement 2x (mAmplifyMovement)
r (hold, lowercase only): enforceIdealPosture + BalanceConstraint OPTIMIZE_BALANCE; release restores FROM_CENTROID
t: reset all DOFs to rest config except root yaw/x/y (indices 2,3,4 preserved)
p: print every DOF name and position to stdout
1-9: toggle IK target InteractiveFrame for EE #(key-1); 6 exist in order l_hand, r_hand, l_foot, r_foot, l_peg, r_peg (keys 1-6)
x: toggle l_foot Support active
c: toggle r_foot Support active
tunables: --gui-scale CLI
no ImGui panel; runtime control via keys only
compile-time: movement steps + 2x shift amplifier, solver max iterations 5, extra_error_clamp 0.1, ground_dist 0.01, posture box bounds, support polygon display elevation 0.05
scenes.interactions: enableDragAndDrop on every Hubo BodyNode (Alt/Ctrl/Shift click semantics from viewer) and on each of the 6 EE InteractiveFrame targets; SupportPolygonVisual (green polygon, blue/red COM, green centroid); printed instruction block mentions '1 -> 6' toggles.
scenes.portRisks: Asset loading uses the DART_DATA_PATH macro rather than dart://sample retrieval — a consolidated app must map this to its own data resolution (drchubo assets do exist under dart data as urdf/drchubo). Skeleton is cloned after EE/IK setup, exercising IK/EndEffector cloning — keep or the demo semantics change. Custom analytical IK classes (~650 lines) live in main.cpp; they lazily mutate skeleton positions during configure() (saved/restored) — thread-unsafe if stepped concurrently. Same shared-viewer risks as atlas_puppet: allowSimulation(false), event-handler key leakage (w/a/s/d/q/e/f/z + uppercase + Shift), SupportPolygonVisual teardown, getEqConstraint(1) index fragility. Uppercase move keys are handled but 'R' is not an optimization key (only 'r'). Kinematic-only demo.

scenes.name: fetch
scenes.worldSetup: World parsed from MJCF: utils::MjcfParser::readWorld("dart://sample/mjcf/openai/robotics/fetch/pick_and_place.xml") (Z-up); collision detector swapped to BulletCollisionDetector on the constraint solver. Robot skeleton "robot0:base_link": root joint(0) actuator LOCKED, every DOF spring stiffness 1e+3, robot0:torso_lift_joint spring stiffness 1e+7; initial base positions dof0=0.405, dof1=0.480, dof2=0.0 and arm positions dofs 6-12 = (0.01,-0.73,0.00,1.64,0.0,0.66,0.01). Object skeleton "object0" placed at (1.25,0.53,0.40) (dofs 3-5). Mocap skeleton "robot0:mocap"; the WeldJointConstraint at solver constraint index 0 (mocap<->EE, from MJCF) has its relative transform reset to Identity. InteractiveFrame "interactive frame" (size 0.2) at (1.3,0.75,0.50) rotated 0.5*pi about Y added as SimpleFrame. GridVisual attachment with offset (1.3,0.75,0). ImGuiViewer (gui scale from CLI), window 1280x960 scaled, camera (4,4,2.5)->(0.1,-0.3,0.3) up (0,0,1). Simulation NOT auto-started (starts paused until Space/Play).
scenes.controllers: FetchWorldNode : RealTimeWorldNode. customPreStep(): guards on robot/mocap/frame non-null, then drives the mocap body kinematically: mMocap->getParentJoint()->setTransformFromParentBodyNode(mInteractiveFrame->getTransform()) — the weld constraint then pulls the Fetch end-effector toward the dragged frame during dynamics. No other control logic; whole-body motion emerges from joint springs + weld constraint. PointCloudWidget (misnamed copy from point_cloud example) is a plain ImGuiWidget: menu (Exit -> setDone, About DART), descriptive text, Help section printing viewer instructions, Simulation header with Play/Pause radio buttons.
keyBindings: (none custom — no osgGA handler added; built-in dart::gui::osg::Viewer defaults apply, e.g. Space: toggle simulate)
tunables: ImGui Play/Pause radio (viewer simulate on/off)
--gui-scale CLI
compile-time: spring stiffness 1e+3 (all dofs) / 1e+7 (torso lift), initial base/arm/object poses, interactive frame pose and size 0.2, grid offset (1.3,0.75,0)
scenes.interactions: viewer.enableDragAndDrop(frame.get()) on the InteractiveFrame — dragging its gizmo moves the invisible mocap target, which the welded end-effector follows during simulation. GridVisual shown at the frame's XY location (transparent green bars cross). ImGui menu/help/play-pause.
scenes.portRisks: Hard dependency on dart-collision-bullet (CMake component collision-bullet) — optional in some DART builds; the demos host must gate this scene on Bullet availability. Fetching the weld constraint by index 0 from the constraint solver is fragile against MJCF parser/constraint-order changes. DART_ASSERT is used for load-failure handling (no-op in release builds — silent nullptr deref if asset missing). Skeleton/joint names hardcoded to the OpenAI MJCF ('robot0:base_link', 'robot0:torso_lift_joint', 'object0', 'robot0:mocap'). RealTimeWorldNode + ImGuiViewer; scene starts paused.

scenes.name: vehicle
scenes.worldSetup: Y-UP world from SkelParser::readWorld("dart://sample/skel/vehicle.skel"); gravity explicitly set to (0,-9.81,0). Skeleton "car_skeleton" with free root (dofs 0-5), steering dofs 6 and 8, wheel spin dofs 7,9,10,11. Plain Viewer (not ImGui), window fixed 640x480 (NOTE: no GuiScaleOptions parsing in this example), camera (5,3,3)->(0,0,0) up (0,1,0). Instruction text added for w/s/x/a/d and space.
scenes.controllers: VehicleWorld : RealTimeWorldNode with mK=0.01, mD=0.005. customPreStep(): reads handler state; tau[6] and tau[8] = -K*(q - steeringAngle) - D*dq (position servo on steering), tau[7],tau[9],tau[10],tau[11] = -D*(dq - backWheelVelocity) (velocity servo on all four wheel-spin dofs), root 6 dofs zero; vehicle->setForces(tau). VehicleEventHandler (osgGA) owns mBackWheelVelocity (set to -420deg/s forward, 0 stop, +420deg/s backward) and mSteeringWheelAngle (+/-10deg increments clamped to +/-30deg), printing each change to stdout.
keyBindings: w / W: move forward (back-wheel target velocity -420 deg/s)
s / S: stop (wheel target velocity 0)
x / X: move backward (wheel target velocity +420 deg/s)
a / A: steer left (+10 deg per press, clamped to +30 deg)
d / D: steer right (-10 deg per press, clamped to -30 deg)
Space: simulation on/off (built-in Viewer default, advertised in instruction text)
tunables: steering angle: +/-10 deg steps, range [-30,+30] deg (runtime via keys)
wheel target velocity: {-420, 0, +420} deg/s (runtime via keys)
compile-time: servo gains mK=0.01, mD=0.005; gravity vector
scenes.interactions: Keyboard only; no drag-and-drop, no ImGui, no mouse picking. Console prints on every command.
scenes.portRisks: Y-up world (gravity and camera up (0,1,0)) — conflicts with a Z-up host grid/camera. DOF indices 6-11 are hardcoded to vehicle.skel joint ordering; any asset change silently breaks the controller. Event handler is shared with the world node by raw pointer — lifetime/teardown coupling on scene switch; keys w/s/x/a/d clash with atlas/hubo teleop bindings. Only example without --gui-scale parsing and with a fixed 640x480 window — parity gap to document rather than replicate.

scenes.name: point_cloud
scenes.worldSetup: World::create() with ZERO gravity, Z-up. KR5 robot from dart://sample/urdf/KR5/KR5 sixx R650.urdf (DartLoader), root joint transformFromParentBodyNode reset to Identity to make Z up, renamed "KR5"; ground from dart://sample/urdf/KR5/ground.urdf, transform pretranslated (0,0,0.5) and rotated +90deg about X. Three SimpleFrames added: "point cloud" (PointCloudShape, point type BOX, RGB blue, color mode set to BIND_PER_POINT in node ctor), "voxel" (VoxelGridShape resolution 0.05, RGBA Orange(0.5), visual shown), "sensor" (SphereShape r=0.05, red). ImGuiViewer with viewer->simulate(true) (auto-plays), GridVisual attachment (defaults). Window 1280x720 scaled; camera (2.57,3.14,1.64)->(0,0,0.30) up (-0.24,-0.25,0.94).
scenes.controllers: PointCloudWorld : RealTimeWorldNode. customPreStep() (skipped when mUpdate false): random-walks robot joint positions (pos += 0.01*VectorXd::Random); builds a 500-point octomap::Pointcloud either SAMPLE_ON_ROBOT (rejection-samples random visual MeshShape vertices via deprecated MeshShape::getMesh assimp scene, transformed to world) or SAMPLE_IN_BOX (uniform in [-0.5,0.5]^3); orbits the "sensor" frame (static local double time, dt=0.001/step, radius 1.0, center (0,0.1,0), z=0.5+0.25*sin(2t)); pushes points into PointCloudShape, computes per-point colors (z-height red-blue gradient, alpha 0.75, clipped 0.1-0.9), and calls VoxelGridShape::updateOccupancy(pointCloud, sensorPos). State: mSampleingMode, mUpdate flag, shape/visual-aspect pointers.
keyBindings: (none custom — no osgGA handler; built-in Viewer defaults, e.g. Space: toggle simulate)
tunables: ImGui Play/Pause radio (viewer simulate)
ImGui Run/Stop Robot Updating radio (node setUpdate)
ImGui sampling mode radio: Sample on robot / Sample in box
ImGui Checkbox 'Point Cloud' visibility (VisualAspect show/hide)
ImGui Combo 'Color Mode': Use shape color / Bind overall / Bind per point (default Bind per point)
ImGui ColorEdit4 'Color' (only when color mode = Use shape color)
ImGui Combo 'Point Shape Type': Box / Billboard Square / Billboard Circle / Point
ImGui InputFloat 'Visual Size' (step 0.01, floor clamped to 0.01)
ImGui Checkbox 'Voxel Grid' visibility + ColorEdit4 'Voxel grid color'

ImGui Grid subsection: Show checkbox; plane radio XY/YZ/ZX; offset X/Y/Z InputFloats (step 0.1); 'Line Count' InputInt (>=0); 'Line Step Size' InputFloat (step 0.001); 'Minor Lines per Major Line' InputInt (>=0); 'Axis Line Width' InputFloat; 'Major Line Width' InputFloat; 'Major Line Color' ColorEdit3; 'Minor Line Width' InputFloat; 'Minor Line Color' ColorEdit3
--gui-scale CLI
compile-time: 500 points/step, robot random-walk amplitude 0.01, sensor orbit params, voxel resolution 0.05
scenes.interactions: No drag-and-drop or custom mouse picking; all interaction through the ImGui panel ('Point Cloud & Voxel Grid Demo', 360x600, menu Exit/About DART, Help prints viewer instructions).
scenes.portRisks: Requires DART built with octomap support (VoxelGridShape + octomap::Pointcloud) — optional dependency; gate the scene on it. Uses deprecated MeshShape::getMesh() wrapped in DART_SUPPRESS_DEPRECATED. customPreStep uses a function-local static time accumulator, and the widget's Grid section uses static locals (offset, cellCount, cellStepSize, etc.) — statistics leak state across scene re-entry and across any second instance in a consolidated app; convert to members when porting. generatePointCloudOnRobot has an unbounded while(true) that never exits if the robot has no visual MeshShapes. maxZ initialized to std::numeric_limits<double>::min() (positive) — subtle color-scale bug for all-negative-z clouds; replicate or document. Widget assumes isAllowingSimulation for parts of the View section.

scenes.name: drag_and_drop
scenes.worldSetup: new World with only SimpleFrames (no skeletons, no stepping content): InteractiveFrame "interactive frame" (size 2.0) at (4,-4,0) parented to World; SimpleFrame "draggable" — red 1x1x1 BoxShape — parented to the InteractiveFrame at relative (-4,4,0); three 0.2 box axis markers X at (8,0,0) red, Y at (0,8,0) green, Z at (0,0,8) blue. Plain WorldNode + plain Viewer. Window 640x480 scaled via --gui-scale; camera (20,17,17)->(0,0,0) up (0,0,1).
scenes.controllers: None — stock dart::gui::osg::WorldNode, no custom pre/post step/refresh, no custom classes at all.
keyBindings: (none custom — built-in Viewer defaults only, e.g. Space: toggle simulate)
tunables: --gui-scale CLI
(no runtime tunables)
scenes.interactions: viewer.enableDragAndDrop(frame.get()) on the InteractiveFrame gizmo and viewer.enableDragAndDrop(draggable.get()) on the red box SimpleFrame (translate by drag; 'Ctrl + Left-click: Rotate the box' added to instruction text). Because the box is a child of the InteractiveFrame, dragging the gizmo also carries the box.
scenes.portRisks: Minimal. Only subtlety is the parent/child relationship (draggable rides the InteractiveFrame) and the SimpleFrame drag-and-drop mode (frame DnD, not BodyNode DnD) — port review should verify both objects remain independently draggable and the instruction text is preserved.

scenes.name: imgui
scenes.worldSetup: new World (empty, default gravity) plus a single default-size InteractiveFrame "target" at Frame::World() added as SimpleFrame. CustomWorldNode : plain WorldNode with empty customPreRefresh/customPostRefresh/customPreStep/customPostStep placeholder hooks (note: declared without override — they do shadow the virtuals correctly since signatures match). ImGuiViewer (heap osg::ref_ptr), TestWidget added to ImGuiHandler, CustomEventHandler added. Window 640x480 scaled; camera (2.57,3.14,1.64)->(0,0,0) up (-0.24,-0.25,0.94).
scenes.controllers: None beyond placeholders. TestWidget (ImGuiWidget) state: mGuiGravity/mGravity bools (setGravity toggles world gravity between -9.81*UnitZ and zero — Z-up assumption), mGuiHeadlights. render(): window 'Tinkertoy Control' 240x320 NoResize, menu bar (Menu->Exit setDone; Help->About DART showAbout); Simulation header with Play/Pause radios (gated on isAllowingSimulation) + world time readout; World Options header with Gravity checkbox and Headlights checkbox (synced from viewer->checkHeadlights each frame); View header printing camera eye/center/up from getViewMatrixAsLookAt; Help header printing viewer->getInstructions().
keyBindings: q (keydown): print 'Lowercase q pressed'; (keyup): print 'Lowercase q released'
Q (keydown/keyup): print 'Capital Q pressed/released'
Left arrow (keydown/keyup): print 'Left arrow key pressed/released'
Right arrow (keydown/keyup): print 'Right arrow key pressed/released'
(all handlers return true, i.e. these keys are consumed; plus built-in Viewer defaults such as Space)
tunables: ImGui Play/Pause radio (viewer simulate)
ImGui Checkbox 'Gravity On/Off' (-9.81 Z vs zero)
ImGui Checkbox 'Headlights On/Off'
--gui-scale CLI
scenes.interactions: viewer->enableDragAndDrop(target.get()) on the InteractiveFrame (drag/rotate gizmo); ImGui panel with menu, simulation control, world options, live camera pose readout, help text. This example is the template/tutorial for WorldNode + event handler + ImGuiWidget wiring.
scenes.portRisks: Low risk; mostly a wiring template. Gravity toggle hardcodes Z-up -9.81. The q/Q/arrow key handler consumes Left/Right arrows which OSG camera manipulators may otherwise use — in a shared host either drop or namespace these demo keys. TestWidget::setGravity dedupes via cached bool, so external gravity changes desync the checkbox. Widget caches ImGuiViewer* and WorldPtr — teardown ordering matters when swapping scenes in one viewer.
